observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant to record the DBP bank
account and its related transactions in the books of accounts, and
to prepare the appropriate adjusting entry to correct the
understatement of CIB balance amounting to ₱41,585.94 as of
year-end;
b. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain a
cashbook for the said bank account to ensure accurate recording
and monitoring of the account; and
c. Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that all bank accounts maintained by the
Municipality are completely and accurately recorded in the books
of account, with the required financial reports prepared and
reconciliations conducted regularly to prevent future
discrepancies.

2.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the OIC
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Establish RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year using
the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and
collectible based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer's Index Cards
submitted by the Acting Municipal Treasurer; and
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the understatement in
the RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the correct balances as of year-
end.

3.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the OIC
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Conduct a comprehensive reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to
eliminate or minimize unreconciled balances and record the
necessary adjusting entries; and
b. Establish standard operating procedures to ensure regular
reconciliation and timely adjustments, enhancing the accuracy and
reliability of financial reporting.

4.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant to prepare the
appropriate adjusting entry to correct the understatement of
inventory amounting to ₱3,595.00 in the FS;
b. Require the Acting General Services Officer to conduct semestral
physical count of inventories and prepare the corresponding RPCI
as mandated, and submit the same to the Audit Team not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively; and
c. Direct the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Acting General
Services Officer to conduct periodic reconciliation of inventory
records with the physical count. Any discrepancies should be
promptly investigated and adjusted to ensure accurate and fair
presentation of inventory accounts.

5.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry
Voucher to record the 10 unrecognized parcels of land of ₱16.988
million and correct the understated value of Lot No. 1065-B-1 by
₱223,660.00;
b. Direct the Municipal Assessor and the OIC Municipal Accountant
to conduct regular coordination and reconciliation to ensure that
land assets are consistently and accurately recorded in both the
accounting and property records;
c. Retrieve, authenticate, and consolidate the original Deeds of
Donation, and initiate the appropriate legal processes for titling in
the Municipality’s name;
d. Coordinate with the Registry of Deeds to secure Certified True
Copies of land titles corresponding to the donated properties; and
e. Provide budgetary allocation for the costs of documentation, legal
processing, and other incidental expenses necessary for the
registration and titling of all unregistered land properties.

6.14 We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
a. The Inventory Committee to:
a.1 Prepare a PIP in coordination with the GSO and Accounting
Office, and submit the PIP approved by the Municipal Mayor
to the Audit Team at least 10 CD before the scheduled start of
inventory taking activities;
a.2 Conduct a comprehensive and timely physical inventory of all
PPE items in accordance with the approved PIP, enlisting
support from technical staff and concerned offices to ensure
completeness, accuracy and proper documentation;
a.3 Reconcile the results of the physical count with the property
and accounting records immediately after the completion of
inventory activities; and
a.4 Prepare and submit a complete and reconciled RPCPPE based
on the results of the actual physical count, to uphold the
integrity of the Municipality's property records, in compliance
with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,
Volume I, not later than January 31 of each year.
b. The OIC Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal
Entry Vouchers to adjust and correct the misstatements in the
affected PPE accounts totaling ₱2,442,862.02, ensuring that
reconciled and accurate balances are reflected in the financial
statements.

7.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the OIC
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Expedite the transfer of completed projects to the appropriate PPE
accounts to ensure an accurate financial position;
b. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to ensure correction of understated
transferred amounts and implement controls to guarantee accurate
recording of all project costs;
c. Enhance internal controls and review procedures to ensure proper
classification of completed projects;
d. Reconcile the accounting records and actual project status of prior year
balances to identify discrepancies and make necessary adjustments; and
e. Strengthen coordination with the Acting-Municipal Engineer to ensure
timely recognition and proper classification of completed projects.

We therefore recommended to the Management the following:

a. Advance the Full Implementation of One-time Cleansing of Property, Plant and
Equipment per COA Circular 2020 – 006 and subsequently direct the General
Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count
and classify properly the accounts presented in the RPCPPE;

b. Instruct the Accounting Office led by the Municipal Accountant to make necessary
adjusting entries for accounts that require reclassification, to reflect a more
accurate balance of subaccount moving forward;

c. Encourage the General Service Office (GSO) and the Accounting Office to
implement regular reconciliation between the accounting records and physical
count reports to ensure timely detection and correction of discrepancies;

d. Direct the GSO and the Accounting Office to conduct a thorough review of the
discrepancies to identify potential causes and implement preventive measures to
avoid future inconsistencies;

e. Require the Accounting Office, through the Municipal Accountant, to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries in order to reconcile the balances per financial
statements against the RPCPPE prepared by the General Services Office in
conformance with COA Circular 2020 -006, and make full disclosures of the related
information in the Notes to the Financial Statements;

f. Require the Accounting Office to adhere to the useful life ranges outlined in the
above-cited COA Circular and ensure that relevant details of the depreciation
policy, including the rationale for selecting the applied useful life figures, are
included in the notes to the Financial Statements for future audit periods. This will
allow for a more effective assessment of the accuracy of depreciation calculations;

g. Initiate disposal of unserviceable items per P.D. 1445, Section 79; and

h. Mandate the General Service Office, through its Head, to develop an appropriate
management system including an extensive recordkeeping process to properly
document, maintain, preserve and update the essential property records. This is to
avoid future lapses and to establish a more structured manner of reconciling data.

We recommended that the management confer and reconcile the amount with the source agencies to ensure the correct balance of the account. Once reconciled, remit or return the unexpended balance of the fund transfers to the source agencies, Bureau of Treasury, or to the specific Local Government Unit as the case may be. Moreover, strictly comply with the rules and regulations pertaining to the grant, utilization and liquidation of the funds transferred to the implementing agencies.

It is further recommended, if warranted, to consider proposing the write-off of the affected accounts pursuant to the criteria and requisites stated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the management:

a. Prepare and process remittance vouchers simultaneously with the payrolls in
order to ensure that salary deductions per payroll are fully accounted for and
completely remitted to the concerned government agencies; and

b. Endeavor to prepare and issue remittance checks on or before the due date of
the contributions or loan repayments, and other mandatory deductions.

We therefore recommended to the Management the following:

1.) Direct a more comprehensive framework with clearly defined actions and
objectives for each project. This will provide a more structured approach, reducing the
need for multiple realignments; and

2.) To address the minor lapses in reporting, require the concerned
department/s to conduct reconciliation of records prior report preparation to ensure a
more reliable data, and a strengthened monitoring and documentation processes.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to post the RCDs daily in the Cashbooks based on the latest issuances of Official Receipts for the year; b. Direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Voucher Entry to record the reconciling items in the Subsidiary Ledgers; and c. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile monthly the Cashbooks and Subsidiary Ledgers—Cash - Local Treasury to ascertain the completeness and correctness of account balances as reported in the books.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Make necessary adjustments in the books to correct the balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts; b. Return the unutilized balances of fund transfers to the Source Agency as stipulated in the MOA, otherwise, remit the same to the National Treasury; and c. Make necessary adjustments regarding stale checks which remained outstanding after six months but within two years from date of its issue.

We recommended and the Management agreed to Direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Observe compliance with the perpetual recording of the supplies and materials recording of the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implemented by administration by recording all purchases under the account “Construction Materials Inventory”; and b. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer/designate a Municipal GSO who is tasked to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same, and to submit a summary of the utilized Construction Materials Inventory monthly to the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to cause the immediate transfer of the unexpended/unobligated QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱4.020 million to a special trust fund using the following Journal Entries: a.1 For General Fund Books: Dr. Subsidy to other Funds xx Cr. Cash in Bank xx; a.2 For Trust Fund Books: Dr. Cash in Bank xx Cr. Trust Liability – DRRMF xx (with SL for the source and year of transfer); and b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF transferred to Special Trust Fund by year of transfer.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to effect the necessary adjusting entries and henceforth, comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 “Prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units”.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all lacking documentary requirements as indicated in Annex K such as detailed engineering drawing/plan, delivery receipts, notice to proceed, DTR, etc.; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to check and verify the completeness of supporting documents on various claims before signing; and c. Direct all concerned officials/employees to process disbursements vouchers only with complete supporting documents and with the approval of proper/authorized officials to establish its validity/propriety, otherwise, the transaction shall be suspended/ disallowed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the documents including MOA on fund transfers from NGAs on the prescribed period; and b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the receipts, utilization requirements and utilization of the fund transfers.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Include the LDRRMFIP in a separate caption, the list of activities/PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF previous years; b. Ensure that the utilization of the STF-LDRRMF is supported with a revised AIP duly endorsed by the MDRRMC in accordance with item 6.5 of NDRRMC- DBM_DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and c. Direct LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit monthly and annual Reports on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LCD to COA auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to install signboards in the prescribed project for every infrastructure project; and b. Comply with the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as specified in Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in all its infrastructure projects.
We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at year end for submission to the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to: 1. Provide the Municipal Accountant with certified list of RPT and SET Accounts Receivables Register/Tax Index as bases of recording the RPT and SET Receivable; and 2. Intensify the collection of RPT/SET Receivable to include delinquent accounts by providing tax amnesty and post at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the municipal hall pursuant to Section 254 of the RA 7160; b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: 1. Establish and record RPT Receivable and SET Receivable, at the beginning of the year, based on the certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index for the current year provided by the Municipal Treasurer using the following entries: Setting-up of RPT/SET Receivable Real Property Tax Receivable/ xxx.xx Special Education Tax receivables Deferred Real Property Taxes xxx.xx Income/ Deferred Special Education Tax Income Receipt of Payment Cash in Treasury xxx.xx Real Property Tax Receivable/ xxx.xx Special Education Tax Receivable Recognition of Income and Distribution of Collection (Current Year’s Collection) Deferred Real Property Tax Income/ Deferred Special Education Tax Income xxx.xx Discount on RPT/SET (for prompt payment) xxx.xx Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax xxx.xx Fines and Penalties xxx.xx Due to LGUs xxx.xx For advance payment of RPT/SET (adjusted on the year the tax is due): Other Assets xxx.xx Deferred Credit to Income xxx.xx

We recommended that the Management: a) Implement the increase in capitalization threshold from ₱15,000.00 to ₱50,000.00 and direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to undertake necessary procedures provided under COA Circular 2022-004; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to reclassify the properties from PPE accounts to Semi-Expendable Property accounts; and c) Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to install measures/ controls over semi-expendable properties such as, but not limited to, update existing PPE and inventory records, and prepare and maintain necessary forms/records/reports for purpose of record keeping of these assets.

We recommended that the Management: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash accounts so that errors/discrepancies be adjusted accordingly, and to exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the accounts; b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to update the balance of collections, deposits and disbursements on all cashbooks daily; c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary entries for the adjustments of affected cash and cash equivalent accounts due to errors/inaccuracies.

We recommended that the Management ensure that Cashbooks are maintained for each fund and bank account and update the balance of the collections, deposits and disbursements on the Cashbooks on a daily basis.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Management: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, DVs and its supporting documents and the bank reconciliation statements to the Commission within the deadline set in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations; and b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the management: a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002; b. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or satisfactory explanation is received within thirty (30) days, cause the withholding of salary due to the accountable officer/s.
observations.reason: There is continuing effort by the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to enforce liquidation; as of April 2025, out of the P128,860.72 cash advance 102,055.44 or 79.20% were already liquidated and demand letters were sent to responsible parties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a) Direct the General Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct
complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the
RPCPPE;

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in
order to reconcile the balances per financial statements against the RPCPPE
prepared by the General Services Office and make full disclosures of the related
information in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

c) Direct the concerned department to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence,
condition and accountability.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Management expressed their intention to conduct the one-
time cleansing of PPE to establish the correct PPE balances. Additionally, the Management
will dispose the remaining unserviceable properties in the succeeding year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a) Implement the increase in capitalization threshold from ₱15,000.00 to ₱50,000.00
and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to
undertake necessary procedures provided under COA Circular 2022-004;

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to reclassify the
properties from PPE accounts to Semi-Expendable Property accounts; and

c) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to install measures/controls over
semi-expendable properties such as, but not limited to, update existing PPE and
inventory records, and prepare and maintain necessary forms/records/reports for
purpose of record keeping of these assets.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular
reconciliation of the cash accounts balances so that errors/discrepancies be
adjusted accordingly, and to exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the
accounts;

b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to update the balance of collections, deposits
and disbursements on all cashbooks daily;

c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary entries for the adjustments
of affected cash and cash equivalent accounts due to errors/inaccuracies.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(LDDRMO) regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Management’s Comment:
The management acknowledged the observation of the audit team and committed to comply
in the submission of required reports pertaining to LDRRM.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, DVs and its
supporting documents and the bank reconciliation statements to the Commission
within the deadline set in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and
regulations; and

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the
timely submission of the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor.

Management Comment:
The management agreed with the audit recommendations and committed to submit all the
remaining DVs and its supporting documents to the Audit Team the soonest possible time.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following:
a) Municipal General Services Officer:
i. Conduct complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the RPCPPE;
ii. Complete the disposal procedures of the remaining unserviceable properties in coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the books of the agency;
iii. Follow the procedures set forth under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the LGU to establish the accountability, condition, and existence of PPEs; and
iv. Institute measures in the monitoring and controlling of the properties of the LGU.
b) Municipal Accountant:
i. Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper adjusting entries to correct the same; and
ii. Ensure that the accounting procedures are done in a timely manner through sound internal controls in order to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), Municipal Accountant and other officers involved in the implementation of the LDRRMF to fully implement the PPAs under the LDRRMF, especially the 70% Mitigation Fund which were allocated for disaster preparedness and to use the 30% Quick Response Fund for the COVID-related emergencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to properly withhold the amount of tax due to the contractor in the projects implemented by the LGU in compliance with the tax laws in the Philippines.
observations.reason: The agency already sent a letter to the contractor to inform and demand payment of the unwithheld tax. However, as of date, no payment has been made.

Reconcile the balances of fund with the source agencies and return the excess or unutilized balances to source agencies concerned. Once reconciled, remit or return the unexpended balance of the fund transfers to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury, as the case may be. observations.reason: The concerned office is in the process of retrieving documents to support reconciliation of unreconciled accounts and unsubstantiated balances; some completed projects' balances were already liquidated and unexpended balances were returned to respective source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accountant will issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant continues to send demand letters and exert effort to enforce liquidation. Some Accountable Officers concerned are no longer connected with the LGU.

disposition and make adjustments in the books to attain accuracy in the presentation
of the financial statements. Moreover, prepare the necessary documents pertaining to
the dormant accounts and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005.

direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a refund to the source agency and close the
bank account with dormant deposit.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:

a. The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to properly
withhold the amount of tax due to the contractor in the projects implemented
by the LGU in compliance with the tax laws in the Philippines.

b. The Local Finance Committee, moving forward, create a well-defined budget
especially in supporting the special projects that the LGU endeavor to
implement to help in achieving efficiency and effectiveness in the allocation of
resources of the Municipality.

c. The Local Government Unit, through the Municipal Engineering Office,
institute remedial action to comply with the required number of furniture and
equipment to be used for the project.

We recommended to the Management the following:

a. Submit a valid justification on the disbursements of loan proceeds from the DBP
without an appropriation ordinance; and

b. Ensure that all disbursements are made in pursuance of an appropriation
ordinance or law as mandated in R.A. No. 7160 and P.D. 1445.

We recommended and agreed on the following: a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall cause the immediate remittance of Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) amounting to ₱431,292.27 to the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013. b. The LCE should direct the Municipal Accountant to: i. Draw accounting journal entries to close the completed projects recorded in the Construction in Progress to corresponding PPE accounts and transfer the completed projects in the books to corresponding PPE accounts. ii. Coordinate with other concerned department heads and determine the status of implementation of various fund transfers and effect their disposition thereof. iii. Reconcile the balances of fund transfers with the source agencies and return the excess or unutilized balances to source agencies concerned. iv. Draw accounting journal entries to correct the entries made on the fund transfers received from the Provincial Government of Cotabato, DILG and DSWD. v. Determine the composition of unreconciled accounts and unsubstantiated balances and make appropriate adjustments in the books to attain fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accountant will issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation; b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; and c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will ensure that elected officials are not granted with cash advances other than those expressly granted by the existing laws, rules and regulations and only permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as disbursing officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and agreed on the following: a. The LDRRM Council should: i. Prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which includes both current appropriations and the unexpended prior year balances. ii. Include in the preparation of LDRRMFIP the establishment of evacuation centers and stockroom for the stockpiling of basic emergency supplies, equipment and early warning device. iii. Judiciously plan the programs, projects and activities to be implemented and utilize optimally the available resources to realize the purpose of the fund. b. The Municipal Accountant should record all procurement of medicines, goods and other supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances. c. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the physical count of inventories. Likewise, the Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor. d. The Bids and Awards Committee should fast track the procurement of the rescue and response equipment.

The LDRRM Council through the MDRRMO will include the establishment of the evacuation center and stockroom in the preparation of LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we reiterated prior year’s recommendation for the inventory
committee to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and immediately reconcile the
report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation of the balances of PPE
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of
the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party
claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.

Require the Municipal Accountant to examine/analyze the discrepancies of the accounts
and thereafter prepare the necessary adjusting/correcting journal entries.

We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to
undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper
implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended
beneficiaries at the soonest time possible.

Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer
should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring
procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to
assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to
allow prompt corrective action.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Barangay Health
Centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the government’s goal
of providing preventive health care services to the indigents and similarly situated constituents will
be optimized thus achieving the fund’s goals and objectives in the most opportune time.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and MPDC Officer comply with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects at
the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required quarterly information of PPAs
of the Municipality.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterate prior year’s recommendation for the inventory committee to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and immediately reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommend to the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPE and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO should gather the relevant documents and complete details of the subject PPE items and take up the necessary adjustments on depreciation and accumulated surplus.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.

We recommend that the Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action. Also, provide budget for Social development which would redound to the benefits of the constituents.

We recommend that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410 by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various activities in support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines on allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 1. s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and to prepare/certify correct the corresponding RPCPPE to be approved by the Municipal Mayor and submit the same inventory report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Page 113 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the accounting records to make necessary adjustments if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended the following: Cause the proper turn-over and transfer of property accountability from one Accountable Officer to another and from an out-going officer to his successor; Conduct of physical inventory of the agency’s property, pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The required annual report shall be certified correct by the committee of three (3) consisting of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman and the general services officer/property custodian designate as the case maybe, and the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned, as members and approved by the Local Chief Executive; Submit in correct format the consolidated RPCPPE as provided in Section 57 and Inventory and the Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59 of NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; Reconcile Property Cards (PC) with the accounting records PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) and prepare the necessary adjustments, if any; and Facilitate the proper turn-over of property accountability between the outgoing Designated Property Custodian and the Incoming Designated Property Custodian as provided in Section 77 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended the following: Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank; Make a representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that Management: Allot a budget for the purchase of a lot suitable for the establishment of a sanitary landfill of the Municipality; and Henceforth, strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source and establishment of a sanitary landfill.

We recommended that Management include priority programs/projects/activities in the annual LSB Budget and charge expenses against the SEF in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 of R.A. We reiterated our recommendations that Management:


a.  Require  the   immediate   liquidation/settlement   by   concerned
   officials/employees of all cash advances  totaling  ₱0.674  million
   as of  December 31, 2016;


b. Cause the withholding of salaries of officials  and  employees  who
   are  still  active   in   service,   effective   immediately,   for
   unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱0.546 million granted in prior
   years particularly for CY 2011; and


c. Henceforth, strictly comply with  COA  Circular  No.  97-002  dated
                        February 10, 1997, COA Circular  No.  2012-004
   dated November 28, 2012 and Paragraph 3.1.2.1 of COA  Circular  No.
   96-004 dated April  19,  1996  on  the  granting,  utilization  and
   liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that  Management  direct  the  Inventory  Committee  to
conduct the required annual physical inventory of  the  Municipality’s
PPE and to prepare/certify correct  the  corresponding  RPCPPE  to  be
approved by the Municipal Mayor and submit the same  inventory  report
to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year  pursuant  to
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Page  113
of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II. The  RPCPPE  should  be
reconciled with the accounting records to make  necessary  adjustments
if there are discrepancies noted.

We recommended the following:


a. Cause the proper turn-over and transfer of property  accountability
   from one Accountable Officer  to  another  and  from  an  out-going
   officer to his successor;


b. Conduct of physical inventory of the agency’s property, pursuant to
   Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386. The required annual  report
   shall be certified correct by the committee of three (3) consisting
   of the representative of the local chief executive as chairman  and
   the general services officer/property custodian  designate  as  the
   case maybe, and the supply accountable officer of the department or
   office concerned, as  members  and  approved  by  the  Local  Chief
   Executive;


c. Submit in correct format the consolidated  RPCPPE  as  provided  in
   Section  57  and  Inventory   and   the   Inspection   Report   for
   Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) pursuant to Section 59 of  NGAS  for
   LGUs, Volume II;


d. Reconcile Property Cards  (PC)  with  the  accounting  records  PPE
   Ledger Card (PPELC) and prepare the necessary adjustments, if  any;
   and
e. Facilitate the proper turn-over of property accountability  between
   the  outgoing  Designated  Property  Custodian  and  the   Incoming
   Designated Property Custodian as provided in Section 77 of  PD  No.
   1445.

We recommended that Management exert effort  to  secure  the  Deed  of
Donation/Usufruct Agreements and proceed with the titling of the  land
in favor of the Municipality  to  establish  ownership.  Refrain  from
constructing concrete structures on untitled land  to  avoid  possible
losses.

We recommended that Management:


a. Require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to prepare the  certified  list
   of taxpayers with the amounts due and collectible  and  submit  the
   same to the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and


b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant  to  establish/draw  a  JEV  to
   record  in  the  Municipality’s  books  of  accounts  the   RPT/SET
   Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income  accounts  as  required  in
   Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:


a. Submit immediately the accounts and reports listed  in  Appendix  I
   which are already overdue; and


b. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 70 and 73 of  the  Manual
   on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 6.05 of COA Circular 95-
   006 dated May 18, 1995 on the  timely  submission  of  reports  and
   records of receipts  and  disbursements  to  facilitate  auditorial
   review.

We recommended that Management require the  Office  of  the  Municipal
Treasurer to stamp  “PAID”  on  the  face  of  all  paid  disbursement
vouchers including all attached  supporting  documents  in  compliance
with COA Circular No. 92-389 dated               November 3,  1992  to
avoid double payment of claims/expenses.

The  observations  and   recommendations   cited   were   covered   by
                                  AOM No. 2016-014 (2016) issued on December 12, 2016.


We recommended that the ICO-Municipal  Treasurer  and  the  Designated
Special Disbursing Officer update their cashbooks  and  balance  daily
the Cash-Local Treasury, Cash-In-Bank and Cash Advances  cashbooks  to
establish cash accountability at the end  of  the  month  and  observe
consistently the ruling, footing and certifying of  the  cashbooks  to
provide accounting control in conformity with Sections 26, 27  and  28
of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that Management submit copies  of  perfected  contracts
and purchase orders to the Audit Team within five (5) days  from  date
of execution or issuance with all the necessary  supporting  documents
and comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular  No.  2009-001
dated February 12, 2009.

Require the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to supervise the preparation and submission of RAAF and the preparation of CRAAF in conformity with Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II to determine the monthly accountability for all accountable forms.

Require the immediate liquidation/settlement by concerned officials/employees of all cash advances totaling ₱1,361,823.16 as of December 31, 2015; Cause the withholding of salaries of officials and employees who are still active in service, effective immediately, for unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱943,887.09 granted in prior years; and Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 and Paragraph 3.1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996, and Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Require the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report of Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund within 15 days after the end of each month duly certified by the Municipal Accountant; Implement programs/activities/projects contained in the approved LDRRMFIP primarily intended for prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, recovery and rehabilitation needs during the current year; and Henceforth, strictly comply with Rule 6, Section 4(25) of IRR of R.A. No. 10121 and Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Expedite the prosecution of 20% development projects identified in the AIP; Create a project monitoring committee to closely monitor the implementation of the identified and prioritized development PAPs funded by 20% DF so that more desired benefits could have been delivered to the constituents as planned; and Henceforth, strictly comply with the provision of Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to immediately return/remit the unutilized balance of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and comply strictly with the ruling of the Supreme Court on PDAF which was declared as unconstitutional.

Direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the missing disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱18,099,221.53 and unsubmitted Report of Check Issued and disbursement vouchers covering the period November to December 2015 amounting to ₱14,219,966.84 to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit; Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to journalize check disbursements based on RCI supported with approved disbursement vouchers; Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to implement adequate measures that will facilitate the timely submission of the financial reports and accounts within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the auditor concerned who shall conduct the necessary examination and audit within thirty days from receipt thereof; and Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 39 (1) (3), 100, and 107 of P.D. 1445, Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
